description:
## The change

fz-5j5.3 silently fixed a real pre-existing bug in
\`cont_key_for_spec\` (\`src/ir_typer.rs\`): the CallClosure branch
handled the fn_constants-resolved slot-0 path but fell back to
\`Descr::any\` for closure_lit-typed closures. The walker
\`walk_spec_for_discovery\` handled both paths via
\`resolve_closure_return\`. Under the old whole-graph-rebuild typer
the disagreement was invisible — both functions ran under the same
wrong logic at every iter — but under fz-5j5.3's split between
worklist (walker) and reachability sweep (cont_key_for_spec), keys
diverged: the sweep computed [any, ...] for cont specs the walker
had registered as [int, ...], orphaning them.

This ticket extracts the fix into its own commit so \`git blame\`
attributes it to "closure_lit cont keying bug" rather than "typer
refactor," and adds a focused regression test.

The fix itself is already in tree (\`src/ir_typer.rs\`,
\`cont_key_for_spec\`, the \`Term::CallClosure\` arm).

## What to add

A focused regression test in \`src/ir_typer_tests.rs\`. Minimal shape:

\`\`\`fz
fn add_to(x), do: fn (y) -> x + y
fn use_it(g) do
  r = g(1)
  r + 100
end
fn main() do
  print(use_it(add_to(7)))
end
\`\`\`

\`use_it\` receives g as an opaque param. Inside, \`g(1)\` is a
CallClosure with a closure_lit Descr (forwarded from add_to's
return). The cont after \`g(1)\` reads \`r\` and adds 100. The
cont's slot-0 must be the lambda's return Descr (int), not \`any\`.
Assert the cont spec's key starts with \`int\`, not \`any\`.

## Out of scope

Don't refactor \`cont_key_for_spec\` further. PROV.6 deletes it
entirely (replaces with provenance-tracked emit/diff).

## Independent of

All other PROV tickets. Lands first to clean repo of bugs before
structural refactor.
title: fz-PROV.1: backfill closure_lit cont_key_for_spec fix as its own commit + test
